title: [APIv2] volume rm force parameter not implement
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
While removing a using volume by force=true, but it doesn't work as expected

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080

2. curl -X DELETE  http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/volumes/test?force=true -v

3. Check the output

**Describe the results you received:**
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> DELETE /v1.40/libpod/volumes/test?force=true HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2020 20:07:22 GMT
< Content-Length: 378
< 
{"cause":"container state improper","message":"error removing container 5f1d5c6c6610e317c48e1ca47489dc8a20926e81d871e1c5c4fe3b2848d1fbb2 that depends on volume test: cannot remove container 5f1d5c6c6610e317c48e1ca47489dc8a20926e81d871e1c5c4fe3b2848d1fbb2 as it is running - running or paused containers cannot be removed without force: container state improper","response":500}

**Describe the results you expected:**
should remove the using volume 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body: @ParkerVR PTAL. Feel free to reach out to me or @jwhonce for help getting started!
Not a Bug - This behavior is intended in order to maintain consistency with `docker volume rm --force` and `podman volume remove -f`, both of which do not remove volumes in use

@ParkerVR That is exactly the purpose of the --force
podman volume rm --help
...
  -f, --force   Remove a volume by force, even if it is being used by a container

man podman-volume-rm
...
       Remove a volume by force.  If it is being used by containers, the containers will be removed first.

@rhatdan I investigated this one with @ParkerVR and @baude last night, and ended up breaking out Docker to see what they do with `--force` so we can match them in the API. The answer is that Docker's `--force` acts absolutely nothing like our `--force`. We assume `--force` means "remove containers, but only if they're not running". Docker takes `force` to mean the same as GNU coreutils `rm` does - "do not error when asked to remove something that does not exist". So `docker volume rm DOESNOTEXIST` will fail, but `docker volume rm --force DOESNOTEXIST` will succeed.

This is very different from how removing images and containers with --force works, and is honestly very confusing. But I think we need to adopt the Docker behaviour, at least on the API side.
We should discuss more in detail at scrum, though.
What happens when a container is using a volume and the user attempts to remove the volume with a force?  It should remove the container and then the volume, is what I would assume.
With Docker, and the Docker API? No, it will not do that. It will error that the volume is in use.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mheon Can we close this issue, Did we remove the ability to pass the force flag?
